channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Minneapolis City Council’s budget committee reduces proposed property tax levy for 2025
item.description: Council members, citing concerns about elevated costs for constituents, approved a series of amendments to the mayor’s proposed budget which would reduce the levy hike by 1.4 percent.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Military leaders are rattled by a list of 'woke' officers that a group urges Hegseth to fire
item.description: The list compiled by the American Accountability Foundation includes 20 general officers or senior admirals and a disproportionate number of female officers.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vance tours Helene damage in North Carolina
item.description: The hurricane struck in late September and caused at least $53 billion in damage in North Carolina, according to a state government estimate.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Vance tells residents in hurricane-stricken North Carolina that they haven't been forgotten
item.description: FAIRVIEW, N.C. (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance on Friday assured residents of western North Carolina still cleaning up from Hurricane Helene that they haven't been forgotten as he surveyed storm wreckage and talked to first responders in one of his first public appearances since the election.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and GARY ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Karine Jean-Pierre peppered with questions about Hunter Biden in first televised press briefing since pardon
item.description: White House press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre was bombarded with questions from reporters in the first televised press briefing since President Biden pardoned son Hunter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state county tees up vote on 'knee-jerk' resolution to protect illegal immigrants from deportations
item.description: San Diego County will vote next week on a resolution to block county cooperation with federal immigration authorities, going even further than California's sanctuary law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rep. Ocasio-Cortez announces bid for top Oversight job as Democrats push for generational change
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez announced a bid Friday to become the top Democrat on the House Oversight Committee, the latest example of generational shakeup that is beginning to ripple across the Democratic Party in the wake of their election losses this year.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: On pardons, Biden weighs whether to flex presidential powers in broad new ways
item.description: President Joe Biden faces a stark choice as he contemplates broad preemptive pardons to protect aides and allies from potential retribution by Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Zeke Miller And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden is considering preemptive pardons for officials and allies before Trump takes office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is weighing whether to issue sweeping pardons for officials and allies who the White House fears could be unjustly targeted by President-elect Donald Trump’s administration, a preemptive move that would be a novel and risky use of the president’s extraordinary c
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP senator announces 'DOGE Acts' to back Musk, Ramaswamy government cost-cutting objectives
item.description: Blackburn announced she will be introducing the "DOGE Acts," following meetings with Musk and Ramaswamy on Capitol Hill on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of prominent veterans sign onto letter supporting 'outstanding' Hegseth nomination amid controversies
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth received a boost Friday from more than 60 retired service members of prominence who defended him against attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daniel Penny to be tapped for Congressional Gold Medal by House GOP lawmaker
item.description: Rep. Eli Crane is planning to introduce a resolution to honor a U.S. Marine veteran on trial for killing a homeless man who was described as acting erratic on the subway.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The first rioter tried on Jan. 6 charges gets reduced prison sentence after Supreme Court decision
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Texas man who was the first rioter to go on trial for the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol was resentenced on Friday to nearly seven years in prison after he delivered an angry, profane rant to the judge who agreed to modestly reduce his original sentence.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez seeks to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) on Friday formally launched a bid to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress — a powerful seat that will play a prominent role in the party's effort to push back against President Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Police Association endorses Trump pick for FBI director
item.description: The National Police Association praised Kash Patel's record of "transparency" and accountability that they said makes him well-positioned to lead the bureau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Incoming GOP senator reveals how he will 'strap rocket-boosters' to Trump's agenda in new Congress
item.description: GOP Sen.-elect Tim Sheehy spoke to Fox News Digital about the issues he plans to prioritize when he is sworn in as a member of the Senate next month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump’s position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC that focused on advertising likening Donald Trump’s stance on abortion to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Boston City Council member is arrested on fraud and theft charges
item.description: A Boston city councilor has been arrested on federal fraud charges, accused of funneling part of an inflated bonus payment to a staffer to whom she was related into her own pockets during an exchange at a City Hall bathroom.
item.dc:creator: Kathy Mccormack, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump nominates Rodney Scott to lead Customs and Border Protection
item.description: Rodney Scott at CBP and Caleb Vitello at ICE would work alongside Stephen Miller, who was named deputy chief of staff for policy and Tom Homan, also tapped to be a "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: France's Macron vows to stay in office till end of term, says he'll name a new prime minister soon
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron vowed Thursday to stay in office until the end of his term, due in 2027, and announced that he will name a new prime minister within days in efforts to overcome the political deadlock following the resignation of ousted Prime Minister Michel Barnier.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US government is closing a women's prison and other facilities after years of abuse and decay
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal Bureau of Prisons is permanently closing its “rape club” women’s prison in California and will idle six facilities in a sweeping realignment after years of abuse, decay and mismanagement, The Associated Press has learned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MICHAEL BALSAMO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden at tree lighting says his holiday wish is for US to continue to seek light, liberty and love
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As he presided over his final Christmas tree lighting at the White House, President Joe Biden on Thursday said his wish for the nation now, and always, was that “we continue to seek the light of liberty and love, kindness and compassion, dignity and decency.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House torpedoes Democratic-led effort to release Ethics report on Matt Gaetz
item.description: House Republicans on Thursday torpedoed a Democratic-led effort to release the Ethics Committee’s report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), sending the resolution back to the panel.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Charlamagne tha God rips Newsom over moves to 'Trump-proof' California
item.description: Radio host Charlamagne tha God ripped California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) over his moves to “Trump-proof” California in the weeks following the president-elect's victory.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic rep pushes Biden to issue blanket pardon to those Patel could target
item.description: He noted that law enforcement officers, military personnel and others are at risk of extreme legal battles.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: Mariah Levison and Rob Fersh help people find consensus on how to solve divisive issues, ranging from prison reform to what art should hang in the Minnesota Capitol. Learn how they do it in this MPR News Talking Sense conversation hosted by MPR News correspondent Catharine Richert.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Head of Democratic Caucus says thin House margins make attendance vital next year
item.description: The head of the House Democratic Caucus is sending an early message to his troops heading into the 119th Congress: Attendance will be crucial given the razor-tight margins in the lower chamber. “It’s important for every member to come to work and to do their job.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth slams 'manufactured media takedown' of him in WSJ op-ed
item.description: Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump's pick to lead the Defense Department, condemned the press for what he described as a "manufactured media takedown" of him. "The press is peddling anonymous story after anonymous story, all meant to smear me and tear me down.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: International students confused, anxious about their futures under second Trump term
item.description: International students are holding their breath as President-elect Trump gears up to return to the White House. Multiple schools have put out guidance on when foreign-born students should and shouldn't go abroad to ensure they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward upholding Tennessee's restrictions on gender-affirming care
item.description: The Supreme Court’s conservatives appeared to lean toward upholding Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors in a blockbuster showdown over transgender rights Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis ordered to turn over communications in Trump case to conservative group
item.description: A Georgia judge ordered Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis to turn over to a conservative watchdog group any communications she may have with special counsel Jack Smith or the now-disbanded House Jan. 6 committee.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5
